supply_chain.dependencies: Multiple sources for raw materials including brass, copper, zinc, acrylic resins, titanium dioxide. Reliance on third-party suppliers for raw materials, components and finished products. Dependence on international suppliers particularly in countries outside the United States.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions due to changing laws and regulations, cybersecurity breaches, labor availability, raw material shortages, energy availability, adverse weather conditions, pandemics, social or civil unrest, wars or conflicts. Increased complexity due to international sourcing. Transportation constraints and port congestion. Supplier business continuity risks.

regulatory_risks: Federal, state, local and international government laws and regulations compliance
Product safety regulations and product recalls
Employment and labor laws including wage and hour matters
Trade regulations including duties and tariffs
Climate change regulations and government response to climate change
ESG (Environmental, Social and Governance) practices and stakeholder expectations
Product certification requirements by industry organizations
New or more stringent future regulatory requirements

sanction_risks: Risks associated with international operations including disruptions in trade relations
Changes in policies of U.S. and foreign governments
